notes: NI Scarbee Clavinet Pianet — sampled Hohner Clavinet D6 and Pianet N in Kontakt. Two instruments in one plugin. The Clavinet D6 has four pickup switches (A, B, C, D) selectable in pairs — each combination produces a distinct tonal character: AB = bright and nasal, CD = warm and round, ABCD = full fat. QC1 cycles through these combinations. The wah (QC3) and envelope filter (QC4) are built-in and highly playable — automate CC 19 for classic Stevie Wonder/Shaft-style clavinet. The Pianet N (the other half of the instrument) has a simpler tone but a distinctive plucked-foam character; QC7 controls the foam release noise that defines it. Both respond to velocity heavily — play hard for aggression.

Modulation.description: Modulation — wah sweep or filter depth (instrument-dependent)

Sustain.description: Sustain pedal — note sustain (Clavinet normally has no sustain pedal; adds unnatural sustain)

Soft Pedal.description: Soft pedal — reduces attack force / mutes slightly

Quick Control 1.description: Pickup Selection — Clavinet D6 pickup switch combination: low = AB (bright/nasal), mid = CD (warm/round), high = ABCD (full). Pianet: pickup level.

Quick Control 2.description: Mute Amount — string/damper mute level; adds percussive, dampened character (essential for funk Clavinet)

Quick Control 3.description: Wah Sweep — built-in wah filter position; automate for classic Stevie Wonder clavinet wah effect

Quick Control 4.description: Envelope Filter Amount — auto-wah envelope depth; how much velocity triggers the filter sweep (funk staple)

Quick Control 5.description: Overdrive Amount — built-in overdrive; even light amounts add the essential Clavinet bite and grit

Quick Control 6.description: Tone — overall tonal character from dark to bright; interacts strongly with pickup selection

Quick Control 7.description: Key / Release Noise — string snap and damper noise on release. Pianet: foam release pad noise (the defining character of the Pianet N).

Quick Control 8.description: Humanise — random velocity and timing variation for a more played-in feel
